_status: machine-drafted v2 scheme, unreviewed — review pass + Aaron line-item pending

playbook_grounding.position: Cause should be broad enough to cover serious misconduct, dishonesty, policy violations, material duty failures, legal violations, and conduct harmful to the company.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Cause includes material failure to perform duties, violation of Company policy, dishonesty, fraud, misconduct, breach of fiduciary duty, violation of law, material breach of the agreement, or conduct that materially harms Company.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if Company can end employment for cause only after an unusually narrow event.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Cause includes material failure to perform duties, violation of Company policy, dishonesty, fraud, misconduct, breach of fiduciary duty, violation of law, material breach of the agreement, or conduct that materially harms Company.

acceptable.text: Cause includes the complete signed list. Fraud, dishonesty, willful misconduct, and other incurable serious grounds permit termination without a cure period. Company provides the written Cause notice within 1 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 2 days.

Cause includes the complete signed list. A curable material duty or agreement breach carries a 10-day cure period; fraud, dishonesty, and willful misconduct remain incurable grounds. Company provides the written Cause notice within 3 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 8 days.

Cause includes the complete signed list. A curable material duty or agreement breach carries a 15-day cure period; fraud, dishonesty, and willful misconduct remain incurable grounds. Company provides the written Cause notice within 5 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 6 days.

Cause includes the complete signed list. Fraud, dishonesty, willful misconduct, and other incurable serious grounds permit termination without a cure period. Company provides the written Cause notice within 7 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 4 days.

unacceptable.text: Cause includes the complete signed list. A curable material duty or agreement breach carries a 5-day cure period; fraud, dishonesty, and willful misconduct remain incurable grounds. Company provides the written Cause notice within 3 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 6 days.

Cause extends to the identified minor nonmaterial event after a 15-day cure period even though it does not materially breach a duty, policy, law, or agreement and does not materially harm Company. Company provides the written Cause notice within 1 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 8 days.

Cause extends to the identified minor nonmaterial event after a 10-day cure period even though it does not materially breach a duty, policy, law, or agreement and does not materially harm Company. Company provides the written Cause notice within 7 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 2 days.

Cause includes the complete signed list. A curable material duty or agreement breach carries a 7-day cure period; fraud, dishonesty, and willful misconduct remain incurable grounds. Company provides the written Cause notice within 5 days after its determination and offers an investigation meeting within 4 days.

playbook_grounding.position: Confidentiality and invention assignment should protect company information and assign work-created intellectual property, while documenting any prior inventions excluded from the assignment.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Executive will protect Company confidential information, assign all inventions and work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources, and list excluded prior inventions on a signed schedule.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if company information or employment-created intellectual property is not protected.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Executive will protect Company confidential information, assign all inventions and work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources, and list excluded prior inventions on a signed schedule.

acceptable.text: Executive assigns inventions and work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources and continues to protect Company confidential information. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 10 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 20 days.

Executive assigns inventions and work product created within the scope of employment or using Company resources and continues to protect Company confidential information. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 15 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 10 days.

The identified prior invention remains excluded from assignment because it appears on the signed schedule; Company confidential information and employment-created work remain protected. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 20 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 40 days.

The identified prior invention remains excluded from assignment because it appears on the signed schedule; Company confidential information and employment-created work remain protected. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 5 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 30 days.

unacceptable.text: Executive excludes the asserted prior invention from assignment even though it is not identified on a signed schedule. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 15 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 40 days.

Executive excludes the asserted prior invention from assignment even though it is not identified on a signed schedule. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 10 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 10 days.

Executive excludes an improvement made during employment because a related background invention appears on the schedule, including the employment-created improvement itself. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 5 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 20 days.

Executive excludes the asserted prior invention from assignment even though it is not identified on a signed schedule. Executive discloses potentially assigned inventions within 20 days and may propose clerical schedule updates within 30 days.

playbook_grounding.position: Any bonus should be discretionary or tied to defined metrics and should not become guaranteed merely because employment continues.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Any bonus is discretionary unless a written bonus plan states objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the agreement promises an automatic or guaranteed bonus.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Any bonus is discretionary unless a written bonus plan states objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing.

acceptable.text: Any bonus remains discretionary, and continued employment alone creates no entitlement. Company reviews reported metrics every 45 days and processes an approved payment within 15 days after certification.

Executive is eligible for a target bonus of 30% only under a signed written plan stating objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing. Company reviews reported metrics every 60 days and processes an approved payment within 10 days after certification.

Executive is eligible for a target bonus of 20% only under a signed written plan stating objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing. Company reviews reported metrics every 15 days and processes an approved payment within 5 days after certification.

Any bonus remains discretionary, and continued employment alone creates no entitlement. Company reviews reported metrics every 25 days and processes an approved payment within 20 days after certification.

unacceptable.text: Executive automatically earns a 20% bonus through continued employment, without objective metrics or further approval. Company reviews reported metrics every 60 days and processes an approved payment within 5 days after certification.

Executive is eligible for a target bonus of 40% only under a signed written plan stating objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing. Company reviews reported metrics every 45 days and processes an approved payment within 10 days after certification.

Executive automatically earns a 30% bonus through continued employment, without objective metrics or further approval. Company reviews reported metrics every 25 days and processes an approved payment within 15 days after certification.

Executive is eligible for a target bonus of 35% only under a signed written plan stating objective metrics, approval requirements, and payment timing. Company reviews reported metrics every 15 days and processes an approved payment within 20 days after certification.

playbook_grounding.position: Equity should be governed by the applicable plan and award documents, with no vesting acceleration or extra grant rights beyond those documents.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Equity awards are governed solely by the applicable equity plan and award agreements, and this agreement does not create additional grants, vesting rights, or acceleration.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if a side clause gives equity rights beyond the plan or award documents.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Equity awards are governed solely by the applicable equity plan and award agreements, and this agreement does not create additional grants, vesting rights, or acceleration.

acceptable.text: This agreement creates no additional grant, vesting, or acceleration beyond the applicable equity plan and signed award documents. Company delivers award notices within 20 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 120 days.

The applicable plan and signed award document expressly authorize acceleration of 50% of the unvested award; no extra-plan equity right is created. Company delivers award notices within 5 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 90 days.

The applicable plan and signed award document expressly authorize acceleration of 100% of the unvested award; no extra-plan equity right is created. Company delivers award notices within 10 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 60 days.

This agreement creates no additional grant, vesting, or acceleration beyond the applicable equity plan and signed award documents. Company delivers award notices within 15 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 30 days.

unacceptable.text: This agreement independently accelerates 50% of the unvested award even though the applicable plan and award document do not authorize it. Company delivers award notices within 5 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 60 days.

This agreement independently accelerates 100% of the unvested award even though the applicable plan and award document do not authorize it. Company delivers award notices within 20 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 90 days.

The applicable plan and signed award document expressly authorize acceleration of 125% of the unvested award; no extra-plan equity right is created. Company delivers award notices within 15 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 120 days.

This agreement independently accelerates 75% of the unvested award even though the applicable plan and award document do not authorize it. Company delivers award notices within 10 days after approval, and any vested-option exercise window stated in the award remains 30 days.

benign_change_templates.text: The parties confirm that employment disputes will proceed through individual arbitration.
The parties confirm that Delaware law governs this agreement.

novel_deviation_templates.text: Company may not terminate Executive before the end of a guaranteed three-year employment term.
